sources_exist.note: All seven canonical URL occurrences resolve, although the Law Practice Exchange page exposes no substantive article text in the accessible response.

figures_match_sources.note: The cited pages do not support any of the seven score-bearing input values as stated. LawNext reports firm-size observations but not the interpolated 27%; the Greenwich report does not establish five active US PE-backed platforms or mention Certum; Peak reports 3.75x-4.34x EBITDA rather than the normalized 3.0x-4.0x range; and the remaining pages do not substantiate the asserted historical trajectory, owner percentage, 6.0x exit multiple, or pricing shares.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: The role-level automation analysis and regulatory moat discussion are directionally plausible, but t50_years=5 is built from an unsupported cloud-adoption timeline and an ad hoc 1.3-year adjustment. pi_dist=0.30 depends on unsupported 65% hourly, 20% contingency, and 15% flat-fee revenue shares, while the owner-age and multiple assumptions driving B and M are not evidenced.

rubric_consistent.note: The deterministic dataset objects match exactly and PE evidence is confined to B/M. However, the active-consolidator count mixes US and UK platforms and includes a company absent from the source; OW applies an estimated small-firm-owner age share and an unsupported succession bonus; and the entry/exit multiples are not sourced for the stated normalized EBITDA and MSO tiers. ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on B, M, S, V is explicitly reviewed; within-tolerance arithmetic differences are not the basis of the verdict.

cross_checks_honest.note: All four mandatory cross-checks are present and candid about billable-hour deflation, client portability, bar-rule constraints, limited comparable transactions, and MSO structuring risk. Their quantitative premises require correction, but the narratives do not conceal the central risks.

source_audits.note: The report documents recent PI-law investments including US transactions involving Uplift/Dudley DeBosier, Pelican/Rafi Law Group, and Fortress/Esquire, plus UK transactions involving Sun/Fletchers and Ufenau/Express Solicitors. It does not mention Certum Group and does not establish the record's count of five active US PE-backed platforms pursuing add-ons in the required segment and window.

Peak reports law-firm EBITDA multiples of 3.75x-4.34x and SDE multiples of 2.44x-2.84x. It does not perform the record's owner-compensation conversion, support a 3.0x-4.0x normalized-EBITDA transaction range, or establish 3.5x as the median actually paid for firms in the $1M-$10M EBITDA band.

LawNext reports 30% overall AI use, 18% among solos, 30% for firms with 10-49 attorneys, and 46% for firms with 100 or more attorneys. It does not report 27% for the record's 20-99 employee acquisition band; that figure is an unmarked interpolation across differently defined firm-size populations.

The URL resolves but exposes no substantive article text in the accessible response. It therefore cannot verify either the quoted traditional-law-firm range or the record's separate 6.0x PE-backed MSO exit value; no size-matched transaction cohort or public comparable supporting that value is provided.

The currently accessible article, updated in 2026, does not report 30% cloud adoption in 2015, 60% in 2021, 94% in 2024, or an eight-year 30%-80% adoption corridor. It provides no dated series from which the record's historical analog or t50_years can be reproduced.

The article supports that nearly 14% of lawyers are over 65 and discusses an aging profession, but it does not report that 22% of Top 200 partners are 61+, that 38% of small-firm owners are 60+, or the record's stated succession-shortage statistics. General lawyer and large-firm-partner demographics do not establish the OW input for owners of acquisition-band firms.

The accessible Thomson Reuters article does not report that 90% of legal dollars use hourly arrangements or the record's small-firm revenue mix of 65% hourly, 20% contingency, and 15% flat fee. It therefore does not support the weights used to derive pi_dist=0.30 or the related pricing cross-check.

reasons: inputs.current_adoption_pct.url (https://www.lawnext.com/2025/03/aba-tech-survey-finds-growing-adoption-of-ai-in-legal-practice-with-efficiency-gains-as-primary-driver.html) reports 18%, 30%, and 46% for specified attorney-count groups, not 27% for firms with 20-99 employees. Re-run with a directly observed, acquisition-band-matched material-use measure or label and justify a reproducible estimate without treating attorney-count groups as employee-count groups.

inputs.historical_analogs.url (https://www.appitsoftware.com/blog/legal-tech-adoption-guide-law-firms-2025) does not contain the claimed 2015-2024 cloud-adoption series or the eight-year 30%-80% corridor. Re-run with dated primary ABA survey observations and derive t50_years mechanically from the documented time axis; do not add an ad hoc 1.3-year friction adjustment.

inputs.owners_60plus_pct.url (https://idaabbott.com/blog/demographics-law-firms-help-partners-plan-retirement/) does not support a 38% age-60+ share for small-firm owners or the asserted succession-shortage facts. Re-run with owner/principal demographics for firms in the acquisition band and direct succession evidence before applying the OW bonus.

inputs.active_consolidators.url (https://greenwichgp.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/GCG-PE-and-the-Consolidation-of-Personal-Injury-Law.pdf) does not mention Certum and mixes US investments with UK platforms rather than establishing five active US PE-backed acquirers. Re-run with a named, geography- and window-consistent inventory of platforms actively acquiring this exact segment.

inputs.buy_mult.url (https://peakbusinessvaluation.com/valuation-multiples-for-a-law-firm/) reports 3.75x-4.34x EBITDA, not the asserted 3.0x-4.0x normalized range or 3.5x observed entry median. Re-run with size-matched closed-transaction evidence on consistently normalized EBITDA.

inputs.exit_mult.url (https://thelawpracticeexchange.com/valuation-multiples-for-law-firm-buyouts-key-insights-for-a-successful-merger/) exposes no auditable article content and provides no support for a 6.0x MSO exit. Re-run with a directly accessible source reporting size-matched MSO transactions or public comparables and a reproducible EBITDA normalization.

inputs.pricing_structure.url (https://www.thomsonreuters.com/en-us/posts/legal/state-of-the-us-legal-market-2025/) does not support either the quoted 90% hourly-dollar claim or the 65%/20%/15% revenue split used in pi_dist. Re-run with acquisition-band billing-revenue evidence for hourly, contingency, and flat-fee work and recompute pi_dist from those observed shares.
flags_reviewed: ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on B, M, S, V
